verses.text: An unjust steward, accused of wasting, schemes to secure friends before dismissal. Shrewd self-interest. He plans wisely, though wrongly, for his future.
An unjust steward, accused of wasting, schemes to secure friends before dismissal. Shrewd self-interest. He plans wisely, though wrongly, for his future.
An unjust steward, accused of wasting, schemes to secure friends before dismissal. Shrewd self-interest. He plans wisely, though wrongly, for his future.
An unjust steward, accused of wasting, schemes to secure friends before dismissal. Shrewd self-interest. He plans wisely, though wrongly, for his future.
He reduces his lord's debtors' bills; the lord commends his shrewdness. Worldly wisdom. The children of this world are wise in their own affairs.
He reduces his lord's debtors' bills; the lord commends his shrewdness. Worldly wisdom. The children of this world are wise in their own affairs.
He reduces his lord's debtors' bills; the lord commends his shrewdness. Worldly wisdom. The children of this world are wise in their own affairs.
He reduces his lord's debtors' bills; the lord commends his shrewdness. Worldly wisdom. The children of this world are wise in their own affairs.
Use mammon to make friends for eternity; 'he that is faithful in that which is least is faithful also in much.' Faithful stewards. Small trusts test us for greater.
Use mammon to make friends for eternity; 'he that is faithful in that which is least is faithful also in much.' Faithful stewards. Small trusts test us for greater.
If unfaithful in mammon, who will trust you with true riches? 'Ye cannot serve God and mammon.' A clear choice. The heart cannot be divided.
If unfaithful in mammon, who will trust you with true riches? 'Ye cannot serve God and mammon.' A clear choice. The heart cannot be divided.
If unfaithful in mammon, who will trust you with true riches? 'Ye cannot serve God and mammon.' A clear choice. The heart cannot be divided.
The covetous Pharisees deride Him; 'that which is highly esteemed among men is abomination... before God.' Reversed values. God judges by the heart, not by show.
The covetous Pharisees deride Him; 'that which is highly esteemed among men is abomination... before God.' Reversed values. God judges by the heart, not by show.
The law and prophets were till John; the law stands, and marriage is binding. Enduring word. God's commandments are not abolished by the gospel.
The law and prophets were till John; the law stands, and marriage is binding. Enduring word. God's commandments are not abolished by the gospel.
The law and prophets were till John; the law stands, and marriage is binding. Enduring word. God's commandments are not abolished by the gospel.
A rich man fares sumptuously while Lazarus, full of sores, lies at his gate. Heedless plenty. He ignores the misery at his very door.
A rich man fares sumptuously while Lazarus, full of sores, lies at his gate. Heedless plenty. He ignores the misery at his very door.
A rich man fares sumptuously while Lazarus, full of sores, lies at his gate. Heedless plenty. He ignores the misery at his very door.
Both die; Lazarus in Abraham's bosom, the rich man in torment, begging a drop of water. Reversed lots. Death overturns the fortunes of this life.
Both die; Lazarus in Abraham's bosom, the rich man in torment, begging a drop of water. Reversed lots. Death overturns the fortunes of this life.
Both die; Lazarus in Abraham's bosom, the rich man in torment, begging a drop of water. Reversed lots. Death overturns the fortunes of this life.
'Son, remember' thy good things; a great gulf is fixed between us. No crossing. The state of the soul after death is fixed for ever.
'Son, remember' thy good things; a great gulf is fixed between us. No crossing. The state of the soul after death is fixed for ever.
He begs Lazarus be sent to his brethren; 'they have Moses and the prophets; let them hear them.' Sufficient warning. Scripture is enough to lead men to repent.
He begs Lazarus be sent to his brethren; 'they have Moses and the prophets; let them hear them.' Sufficient warning. Scripture is enough to lead men to repent.
He begs Lazarus be sent to his brethren; 'they have Moses and the prophets; let them hear them.' Sufficient warning. Scripture is enough to lead men to repent.
If one rose from the dead they would repent; 'neither will they be persuaded, though one rose.' Hardened. Those deaf to Scripture would not heed a miracle.
